meanings.definition: to be easily noticed or clearly better than others
examples: She *stands* *out* because of her confidence.
This CV will *stand* *out* among the others.

meanings.definition: to perform or complete a task or plan
examples: The experiment was *carried* *out* successfully.

meanings.definition: to appear or happen unexpectedly
examples: An issue *came* *up* during the meeting.

meanings.definition: to be mentioned
examples: His name *came* *up* in the discussion.

meanings.definition: to draw attention to something
examples: She *pointed* *out* a flaw in his argument.

meanings.definition: to manage with difficulty
examples: He earns just enough money to *get* *by* .

meanings.definition: to organize or arrange things
examples: I need to *sort* *out* my closet this weekend.

meanings.definition: to resolve a problem or difficulty
examples: We need to *sort* *out* the issue with the software.
a huge mess to *sort* *out*

meanings.definition: to classify or separate items into groups
examples: They *sorted* *out* the recyclables from the trash.

meanings.definition: to deal successfully with a difficult situation or problem
examples: She has learned to *cope* *with* stress at work.

meanings.definition: to return to the original situation after many changes or events
examples: After many years working abroad, his career has *come* *full* *circle* .
Fashion trends often *come* *full* *circle* .

come full circle.translation: revenir au point de départ, boucler la boucle

meanings.definition: was present at an event, meeting, class, or activity
examples: I *attended* a cybersecurity conference last year.
She *attended* the meeting with the project manager.

attended.translation: a assisté à, a participé à

meanings.definition: to add something extra, often unexpectedly or inconveniently
examples: The boss *tacked* *on* an extra presentation to the meeting.
Don’t just *tack* *on* tasks without letting the team know.

tack on.translation: ajouter, rajouter (souvent de manière inattendue ou gênante)

meanings.definition: to make a temporary arrangement or plan that can be changed later
examples: We *penciled* *in* the annual self-appraisal- for next Monday. (temporary arrangement)

meanings.definition: to be busy or occupied
examples: She’s *tied* *up* in a meeting at the moment.

meanings.definition: to bind or fasten something physically
examples: He *tied* *up* the packages with string.

meanings.definition: to make someone feel sad or depressed
examples: Don’t let this situation *get* *you* *down*.

meanings.definition: to start doing something seriously
examples: It’s time to *get* *down* *to* business.

meanings.definition: to move downward or lower yourself
examples: She *got* *down* on her knees.
The cat *got* *down* from the table.

meanings.definition: to write something down
examples: Did you *get* *down* everything the teacher said?
I *got* *down* the key points from the meeting.

get down.translation: déprimer ; se mettre à ; descendre ; noter

meanings.definition: to have a good relationship with someone
examples: I *get* *on* well with my colleagues.
She doesn’t *get* *on* *with* her manager.
They *get* *on* really well .

meanings.definition: to make progress or continue doing something
examples: How are you *getting* *on* with your project?
We need to *get* *on* with the work.

meanings.definition: to board or enter a form of transport
examples: She *got on* the train just in time.

meanings.definition: to have a good relationship with someone
examples: I *get* *along* *well* *with* my coworkers.

meanings.definition: to manage or make progress in a situation
examples: How are you *getting* *along* in your new job?

meanings.definition: being located immediately beside someone or something
examples: He was *standing* *next* *to* his car.

meanings.definition: to end a phone call
examples: She *hung* *up* *the* *phone* after the conversation.

meanings.definition: a situation or place that is very disorganized or chaotic
examples: The kitchen was *a* *huge* *mess* after the party.
My schedule this week is *a* *huge* *mess* with too many meetings.
a huge mess to sort out

meanings.definition: past participle of awake; to rouse from sleep
examples: I was awoken by a loud noise.

meanings.definition: put or hidden underground
examples: The treasure was buried underground.
sometimes when you are in the dark place , you think you have been buried but actually you've been plainted

meanings.definition: target , the person or thing that is the object of criticism or ridicule
examples: your company has started to become the butt of bad jokes at conferences and in the press.

meanings.definition: the work of a joiner, especially in making furniture or fittings
examples: He works in joinery and makes custom cabinets.

meanings.definition: to include a variety of things between two limits
examples: The prices *range* *from* $10 *to* $100.

meanings.definition: the leading or most important position in a particular field or activity
examples: The company is at the forefront of technological innovation.

meanings.definition: a formal offer to provide goods or services, especially in response to a request
examples: We might then have been better placed in the *tender* evaluations.

meanings.definition: gentle, kind, or caring
examples: She gave him a *tender* smile.

meanings.definition: soft and easy to chew
examples: The meat is very *tender* .

meanings.definition: before the expected or usual time
examples: I arrived *early* for the meeting.

meanings.definition: almost, but not completely
examples: I *nearly* missed the bus.

meanings.definition: to evaluate or estimate the nature, ability, or quality of something
examples: The committee must assess the relative importance of the issues.

meanings.definition: to calculate or estimate the value or cost of something
examples: The damage was *assessed* at £5 billion.

meanings.definition: to achieve or complete a task, duty, or role satisfactorily
examples: The company *fulfilled* its promise.

meanings.definition: to take money out of a bank
examples: I *withdrew* cash from the ATM.

meanings.definition: to remove or stop participating
examples: The team *withdrew* from the competition.

meanings.definition: to lift something from a surface
examples: She *picked* *up* the pen from the table.
He *picked* *up* his bag and left the room.

meanings.definition: to collect someone or something from a place
examples: I will *pick* *you* *up* at the airport tomorrow.
She *picked* *up* her child from school.

meanings.definition: to learn something without formal instruction
examples: He *picked* *up* Spanish while traveling.
You must have *picked* *up* lots of valuable experience

meanings.definition: to improve or increase
examples: Sales *picked* *up* last month.
The wind *picked* *up* in the evening.

meanings.definition: to cause a feeling or emotion
examples: The speech *aroused* strong emotions.
something about the man *aroused* the guard's suspicions  (evoke)
I was surprised to find that this look *aroused* me.(excite)

meanings.definition: to try to do something, especially something difficult
examples: She *attempted* to climb the mountain.

meanings.definition: to hit something hard
examples: Someone *knocks* at the door

meanings.definition: to include or affect someone or something
examples: The project *involves* several departments.

meanings.definition: to develop gradually, especially from a simple to a more complex form
examples: The company has *evolved* over the years.

meanings.definition: to think or believe something
examples: I reckon it's going to rain today. (to think)

meanings.definition: to calculate or estimate something
examples: The cost is *reckoned* to be around $100. (to calculate)

meanings.definition: to cause someone to believe something that is not true
examples: False information can *mislead* people.
Often people are *misled* when they come out of an engineering school for example !

meanings.definition: to continue to be in the same state or condition
examples: She *remained* calm during the interview.
The problem *remains* unsolved.

meanings.definition: to tell someone about a possible danger or problem so they can avoid it
examples: She *warned* me about the heavy rain.

meanings.definition: to extend over a large area or to cover a wide range
examples: The news of the event spreads quickly.

meanings.definition: additional or more
examples: No further action is required.

meanings.definition: to help something develop
examples: This course will further your career.
the research *furthers* our understanding of AI.

meanings.definition: at a greater distance
examples: The village is further than I thought.

meanings.definition: to take or carry something or someone to a place
examples: Please *bring* your documents to the meeting.
She *brought* her laptop to the meeting.

meanings.definition: to cause something to happen
examples: This change will *bring* new opportunities.
The new policy *brought* positive changes.

meanings.definition: a large piece of cloth used on a bed
examples: She changed the bed *sheets* .

meanings.definition: writing and office materials
examples: She bought new *stationery* for school.

meanings.definition: a place where products are displayed for customers to see and buy
examples: The car showroom has the latest models on display.

meanings.definition: a set of steps leading from one floor to another
examples: The building has two staircases for emergency exits.

meanings.definition: the place where an event happens
examples: The concert venue can hold 5,000 people.

meanings.definition: an event where companies present and promote their products or services
examples: The togo 2000 *trade* *fair* is one of the most well-know in togo

meanings.definition: a formal summary of the aims and values of a company or organization
examples: The company's *mission* *statement* emphasizes its commitment to quality.

meanings.definition: benefits received in addition to regular pay
examples: Health insurance is one of the job perks .

meanings.definition: a person who buys and sells stocks on behalf of clients
examples: He works as a stockbroker at a financial firm.

meanings.definition: a mistake caused by not noticing something
examples: Forgetting to attach the file was an *oversight* .

meanings.definition: supervision or monitoring of a process or activity
examples: The project is under government *oversight* .

meanings.definition: a material or substance
examples: What is this *stuff* made of?

meanings.definition: a person's ability to cope well with difficulties; courage and determination
examples: The competition tested his *mettle* .

meanings.definition: an evaluation that you make about your own performance, skills, or progress
examples: Employees must complete a *self-appraisal* before their annual review.

meanings.definition: a large number of people gathered together
examples: Fans *crowded* the streets.
Stand out from the crowd

meanings.definition: a meeting of people
examples: There was a family gathering .

meanings.definition: the act of collecting or accumulating something
examples: The gathering of data is important for research.

meanings.definition: pieces of evidence used to solve a problem or crime
examples: The police found new clues .

meanings.definition: a citation or repeated statement
examples: He ended his speech with a famous quote .

meanings.definition: a statement of the price of something
examples: The quotation for the project was higher than expected.

meanings.definition: a group of words taken from a text or speech and repeated by someone other than the original author or speaker
examples: The book is full of *quotations* from famous authors.

meanings.definition: a plan or program of action
examples: The government has launched a new scheme to support small businesses.

meanings.definition: a task or project that is taken on
examples: The construction of the new bridge is a major undertaking .

meanings.definition: the reason for which something is done or created
examples: The purposes of the project are to improve efficiency and reduce costs.
medical purposes
educational purposes

meanings.definition: the way in which something is arranged or organized
examples: The layout of the office is designed to encourage collaboration.

meanings.definition: the money paid for public transportation
examples: Bus *fares* have increased.

meanings.definition: the way a person acts or behaves
examples: His *behavior* during the meeting was unprofessional.
you said you want to succeed, but your *behavior* says the opposite  (person acts)

meanings.definition: limits or borders that define what is allowed or where something ends
examples: She set clear *boundaries* with her colleagues.
The map shows the *boundaries* between countries.

meanings.definition: a feeling of comfort or relaxation after stress, worry, or pain disappears
examples: It was a great *relief* to finish the task on time.
This cream gives quick *relief* from itching .

meanings.definition: not willing or hesitant to do something
examples: He is quite *reluctant* to share his personal stuff.

meanings.definition: happening after something else
examples: All *subsequent* meetings were postponed.
The initial test failed, but *subsequent* attempts were successful.

meanings.definition: survive a difficult situation
examples: The company *weathered* the financial crisis.

meanings.definition: worn or damaged by exposure to weather
examples: The house had *weathered* wooden doors.

meanings.definition: having a balanced set of skills, qualities, or experiences
examples: She is a *well-rounded* candidate with both technical and communication skills.
The university aims to develop *well-rounded* students.

meanings.definition: acting without thinking about the risks or consequences
examples: Driving too fast in the rain is *reckless* .

meanings.definition: relating to a trial period during which someone's performance or behavior is evaluated
examples: you have only been in the job for a couple of months so I suppose you haven't really finished your probationary period yet

meanings.definition: right or appropriate for a particular purpose or situation
examples: This candidate seems suitable for the position.
It’s probably not a good idea to start complaining too much as they might decide that you are not suitable for the job!

meanings.definition: happy or satisfied with something
examples: She was *pleased* with the results of her exam .

meanings.definition: a very large number of spreadsheets; too many to count
examples: The accountant had to review *countless* spreadsheets before the audit.

meanings.definition: causing difficulty , hard to do or deal with or embarrassment
examples: It was an awkward situation.
an awkward task

meanings.definition: strange or unusual
examples: That's an odd way to solve the problem.

meanings.definition: make or become unclear or less distinct (an action that causes something to become less clear or less focused)
examples: The image is blurred.
the shared screens are blurred
the lines of the painting are blurred

meanings.definition: something that is by nature unclear or not in focus (a state or condition of being unclear or not sharp)
examples: The photo is blurry .

meanings.definition: not just or equitable; not treating people equally
examples: The decision was unfair to the employees.

meanings.definition: not careful or neat in doing something
examples: The sloppy handwriting made it hard to read.

meanings.definition: clean, tidy, and well organized
examples: Her desk is very *neat* .

meanings.definition: complete, careful, and detailed
examples: She did a *thorough* job cleaning the house.
But maybe because of my lack of experience, I hadn’t been *thorough* enough.

meanings.definition: before a particular time or event
examples: Please read the instructions *prior* to starting the exam.

meanings.definition: consistently good in quality or performance; able to be trusted
examples: She is a reliable employee who always meets deadlines.

meanings.definition: able to be related to or understood by someone
examples: The character in the movie was relatable to many viewers.

meanings.definition: dishonestly arranged or manipulated to produce a desired result
examples: The competition was *rigged* , so the result was unfair.
The elections were *rigged* , and it’s unfair- for the whole class.

meanings.definition: prepared or set up in advance
examples: The equipment was *rigged* before the event started.

meanings.definition: extremely good or impressive
examples: The performance was *outstanding*.

meanings.definition: not yet completed or paid
examples: There are several *outstanding* tasks to complete.

meanings.definition: a feeling on the skin that makes you want to scratch
examples: This sweater is *itching* my skin.

meanings.definition: having a strong desire to do something
examples: I’m *itching* *to* start this new project.

meanings.definition: used to introduce a contrast (similar to although)
examples: *Though* he was tired, he kept working.

meanings.definition: used at the end of a sentence to add contrast (informal use)
examples: He’s strict. He’s fair, *though* .
Not an experience I’d care to repeat *though*

meanings.definition: used to introduce a contrast with something that was just said
examples: The project was challenging. *Having* *said* *that* , *though* , we learned a lot.
The job is demanding. *Having* *said* *that* , it is very rewarding.

meanings.definition: used to indicate that a list continues with similar items
examples: I bought apples, bananas, oranges, *and* *so* *on* .

meanings.definition: certainly not; not at all
examples: He is *by* *no* *means* the best player on the team.

meanings.definition: used to emphasize a statement or confirm something
examples: She’s very talented. *Indeed* she is.
*Indeed* . But presumably you’d checked out the firm prior to joining.

meanings.definition: at the beginning of something
examples: *At* *the* *outset* of the project, we set clear goals. (at the beginning)

meanings.definition: used to refer to any person who does something
examples: Whoever is responsible for this mess needs to clean it up.

meanings.definition: used to indicate that any thing or amount is acceptable
examples: You can take whatever you need.

meanings.definition: used to indicate that any one of a group of things is acceptable
examples: You can choose whichever color you like.

meanings.definition: used to add emphasis in questions or exclamations, showing surprise, confusion, or urgency
examples: Who *on* *earth* left this mess ?
why *on* *earth* did you make this choice ? it's by no means the wrong.

on earth.translation: diable, bon sang (pour insister dans une question/exclamation)

meanings.definition: in general, considering everything
examples: *Overall* , the project was successful. (in general)

meanings.definition: general or total
examples: The *overall* cost of the project is high. (total)

meanings.definition: to have been useful or beneficial in a particular situation
examples: My programming skills *stood* *me* *in* *good* *stead* during the technical interview.
His prior experience *stood* *him* *in* *good* *stead* when he applied for the promotion.
The English practice I did last year *stood* *me* *in* *good* *stead* during the exam.
Her training *stood* *her* *in* *good* *stead* in a stressful situation.

stood me in good stead.translation: m’a été très utile, m’a bien servi

meanings.definition: something that is certain or assumed to be true
examples: Hard work is *a* *given* in any successful career.

a given.translation: une certitude, quelque chose de sûr

meanings.definition: talking about a problem with someone makes it easier to deal with
examples: You should talk to someone about your worries *a* *problem* *shared* *is* *a* *problem* *halved* .

a problem shared is a problem halved.translation: un problème partagé est à moitié résolu

meanings.definition: used to ask how likely something is to happen, often expressing surprise
examples: What are the *odds* of meeting you here in Paris?
He guessed the correct number what are the *odds* ?

what are the odds.translation: quelles sont les chances, c’est peu probable

meanings.definition: to deal with difficulties or problems without being upset
examples: He faced criticism at work and took it *in* *his* *stride* .

in my stride.translation: avec sérénité, sans se laisser perturber, avec philosophie

meanings.definition: informal expression meaning someone’s face is considered ugly or unattractive
examples: Some old cartoons exaggerated villains with *faces like a boot*.

meanings.definition: to achieve the desired result or to be successful in doing something
examples: The marketing campaign *hit* *the* *mark* and increased sales.

meanings.definition: to delay doing something intentionally or be slow because you do not want to do it
examples: My meighbor *drags* *their* *feet* to pay their rent (to delay doing something intentionally)
Don’t *drag* *your* *feet* we need to finish this today . (to delay doing something intentionally)

meanings.definition: to fail completely, especially for a business to close or go bankrupt
examples: The startup *went* *belly* *up* after just one year.

meanings.definition: a lot of trouble, stress, or conflict
examples: That decision caused *a* *lot* *of* *aggro* in the team. (a lot of trouble)

a lot of aggro.translation: beaucoup de problèmes, de stress ou de conflits

meanings.definition: to become very angry or lose your temper
examples: The CEO *did* *his* *nut* when he heard about the oversight.

do your nut.translation: se mettre très en colère, péter les plombs

meanings.definition: to indicate possession
examples: I’ve *got* a new phone.

meanings.definition: to indicate having received something
examples: I’ve *got* your email.

meanings.definition: to indicate obligation or necessity (must)
examples: I’ve *got* to finish this report.

meanings.definition: to indicate understanding or realization
examples: I’ve *got* the solution to this problem.
Oh, I’ve *got* it now!

meanings.definition: to indicate that something is completed or sorted
examples: She’s *got* the schedule sorted.

meanings.definition: used to say that something or someone is annoying, inconvenient, or bothersome
examples: What a *nuisance* ! The printer is jammed again.

what a nuisance.translation: quel casse-tête, quelle nuisance, quelle corvée

meanings.definition: Usally used to ask for permission
examples: *Do* *you* *mind*  if we take a break ? (ask for permission)

meanings.definition: Help to do something , give someone a hand
examples: After school , i *gave* *a* *hand* at home (give someone a hand)

meanings.definition: to forget something temporarily
examples: The project deadline has been fixed for late March, so the TVM declaration *slipped* *my* *mind* .

meanings.definition: to examine or check something quickly
examples: Let’s *take* *a* *look* at the report before the meeting.

meanings.definition: if necessary, even though it is not ideal
examples: This room can hold 10 people *at* *a* *pinch* .
My money can buy a Maybach or an S-Class *at* *a* *pinch* .

meanings.definition: to ask someone for advice, ideas, or information
examples: Hey! Can i *pick* *your* *brain* for a minute

meanings.definition: to forget something temporarily
examples: The project deadline has been fixed for late March, so the TVM declaration *slipped* *my* *mind* .

meanings.definition: immediately or without delay
examples: I’ll call you *straight* *away* .
Please fix this issue *straight* *away* .

meanings.definition: inside the limits of something
examples: The answer is within the book.

meanings.definition: in the direction of something or someone
examples: She walked towards the park.

meanings.definition: only just; almost not
examples: She barely passed the exam.

meanings.definition: very nearly but not exactly or entirely
examples: The project is almost complete. (very nearly- )
